title: artemisia vulgaris var. indica
intro: Artemisia vulgaris var. indica, also known as Indian wormwood, is a variety of the common wormwood plant that is native to the Indian subcontinent and other parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial properties. The health benefits of Indian wormwood include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and even treat malaria, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Indian wormwood can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
